name: Vineet Kushwaha
aboutme14: with a strong grip on React, Node.js, and everything in between.
aboutme16: responsive, real-world applications that are easy to use and fun to make.
aboutme17: Alongside web dev, I use Python for automating tasks, experimenting with ideas, and working on computer vision projects. I'm also studying Data Science, which adds some extra perspective to how I solve problems and think about code. I’m still learning, but I try to build with intention and keep getting better with every project.

list.title: SAC Commenting
list.description: Developed an embedded React widget consuming SAP Analytics Cloud postMessage events to capture dashboard context. Built Express.js APIs for comment storage, integrated Vertex AI for contextual JSON summaries, and implemented Puppeteer for automated snapshot generation.

list.title: iConnect 2.0
list.description: Architected a microservice topology separating a Node.js/Express gateway from a FastAPI AI inference agent containerized on GCP Cloud Run. Secured inter-service OAuth 2.0 communication, designed BigQuery generation engines, and implemented transactional caching to reduce redundant Vertex AI invocations by 35%.

list.title: Real-Time Hand Gesture MIDI Synthesizer
list.description: Developed a rule-based coordinate geometry classifier using MediaPipe landmark vectors to recognize 10 finger-count gestures with zero ML execution overhead. Optimized browser execution to achieve 60 FPS via frame skipping and offloaded synthesis to Web Workers, minimizing audio latency to ~12 ms.

list.title: SkillMatrix
list.description: A full-stack learning platform supporting role-based access, course creation, and multimedia content delivery (text, PDFs, video). Designed backend APIs and database schema to handle user roles, enrollments, and content management.

list.title: Endxiety
list.description: An AI-assisted mental wellness platform featuring anonymous chat, emotion tracking, and journaling. Focused on privacy-aware design, state management, and integrating LLM-based insights into user workflows.

list.title: Saarthi AI
list.description: Multilingual scheme recommender using AI, accessible via voice and text.

list.title: BiblioVerse
list.description: An AI-driven ebook recommendation system combining big-data filtering with a modern web interface. Implemented data pipelines using Hadoop and PySpark, and integrated recommendations into a full-stack Next.js application.

commits.desc: Engineered automated validation pipeline processing 400K+ monthly multi-market records, replacing manual workflows and reducing intervention by 90%.
Designed and deployed containerized Node/Express microservice on GCP Cloud Run with secure IAM service account mesh communication.
Integrated Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite features to design Express API streaming interaction metadata into BigQuery for real-time engagement analytics.
Developed secure REST endpoints with HttpOnly cookies, bcrypt hashing, and schema-level validation using Zod.
Led team of 4 developers driving technical decisions, task allocation, project planning, and frontend/backend feature delivery.
Designed and integrated full-stack features using React, Node, Express, and MongoDB collaborating across the lifecycle.
Created mockups, social designs, and brand style guides using Figma and Adobe Suite.
Studying algorithms, data structures, machine learning models, and database engineering.
Led content operations, copywriting, and marketing materials for the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Cell.

list.details: Content Head: Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Cells (IEDC) Acharya Institute of Technology

intro14: . I’m a web developer and a data science student- but mostly, I’m just someone trying to make sense of the chaos, one line of code at a time.
intro21: Between pixels and Python, I’ve learned that building things-apps, ideas, futures-
intro22: isn’t just about logic, it’s about heart.
intro23: Every bug fixed is a tiny win, every late-night debug session a quiet act of belief.
intro31: I don’t have all the answers (yet), but I’m here-learning, breaking, rebuilding.
intro33: But when it runs… it’s kind of beautiful.
